title: Transactional operator decisions
goal: Replace race-prone check-then-write question and replan transitions with typed compare-and-set StateStore commands that mutate state and append the matching event atomically.
acceptance: Use behavioral TDD: first add deterministic concurrent daemon-RPC and StateStore tests for answer-vs-timeout and accept-vs-reject/defer/auto-accept races, observe the invalid double-transition failure, then implement.

Worker-question answer and timeout are compare-and-set commands requiring the expected pending state and token/attempt identity; the state mutation and typed event append occur in one SQLite transaction.

Replan accept, reject, defer, timeout, and budgeted auto-accept share one transactional decision command that enforces exactly one terminal decision and atomically records budget changes plus event evidence.
Commands return typed applied, already-applied-idempotently, conflict, stale-token, and not-found outcomes so daemon RPC methods do not infer correctness from follow-up reads or error strings.

Concurrent handling allowed by the daemon cannot produce two successful answers/decisions, duplicate authoritative events, negative/double budget consumption, or a response that contradicts durable state.
SQLite connections consistently apply foreign keys, WAL policy, a bounded busy timeout, and transaction mode through one connection-configuration seam rather than repeated ad-hoc PRAGMAs.
Attention delivery happens only after the authoritative transaction and remains non-fatal; failed delivery cannot roll back or alter the operator decision.
Tests use real concurrent connections/RPCs and cover duplicate identical commands, conflicting commands, timeout races, stale attempt tokens, restart reads, and event/state agreement.
must_ask_if: The implementation would serialize correctness only with the in-process daemon mutex rather than enforce it in durable state.
An operator decision would become dependent on Herdr/Pi delivery.
Backward-compatible reading of existing question/proposal rows cannot be retained.
Tests would need sleeps as the only concurrency control instead of barriers or deterministic hooks.
